channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump imposing 50% tariffs on certain Canadian goods over alleged trade discrimination
item.description: Canada's relationship with the U.S. has frayed amid Trump's tariff-heavy agenda and vocal displeasure with the nations' trilateral trade pact with Mexico.

item.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iran will pay for killing U.S. service members
item.description: Oil prices have surged around 20% this month as fighting between the U.S. and Iran has sharply escalated.

item.title: The FDA made a big reversal in its cyclosporiasis probe, but it still thinks iceberg lettuce is to blame
item.description: The FDA's investigation into the source of the cyclosporiasis outbreak is causing confusion as companies and consumers alike navigate new updates.

item.title: Trump says Netanyahu won't be arrested in New York, pushing back on Mamdani
item.description: Israel's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, is the target of an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Court related to the war on Gaza.

item.title: Paramount and Warner Bros. merger hit with temporary restraining order
item.description: A California judge granted a temporary restraining order as part of the lawsuit brought by state attorneys general due to antitrust concerns.

item.title: Tech stocks to buy after the big semiconductor pullback, according to UBS
item.description: UBS listed companies with share prices that have fallen sharply even as underlying fundamentals remain resilient.

item.title: Jamie Dimon says markets underestimate risks and he wouldn't buy stocks or Treasurys at current prices
item.description: Comments from JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon contrast with investors' recent willingness to look past wars, tariffs and other shocks.

item.title: Trump administration's head of AI safety agency resigns after 3 months on job
item.description: Arvind Raman, the director of National Institute of Standards and Technology, will serve as acting director of CAISI, according to a spokesperson

item.title: Sen. Darline Graham running for full term to replace late brother Lindsey Graham
item.description: Sen. Darline Graham enters an increasingly crowded Republican field to fill the seat of her late brother Lindsey Graham, who died earlier this month.

item.title: Could ‘The Odyssey’ lead the 'apes' back home to AMC shares?
item.description: AMC options surged out of the gate this morning with over 300,000 contracts traded as of writing.

item.title: FIFA emerges as the $9 billion winner as the biggest, brashest World Cup ever draws to a close
item.description: The football governing body presided over the most lucrative sporting event in history and is poised to net over $9 billion in revenue for 2026.

item.title: World Cup 2026 final in photos: Spain triumphs as football crowns a new champion
item.description: Spain reclaimed football's biggest prize on Sunday, defeating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in a tense World Cup final.

item.title: Jim Cramer says it's time to look beyond tech as AI uncertainty rattles the market
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer says the AI trade has become too unpredictable, making this a better time to put new money into high-quality companies outside of technology.

item.title: These dividend-paying data center power plays are on Goldman’s latest top 10 list
item.description: Goldman Sachs updated its "Ten Buys" list of energy and power stocks. These two dividend payers are linked to the data center trend.

item.title: Tuesday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: A turn higher in oil prices kept stocks under pressure on Monday. The Dow Industrials slid more than 300 points as Apple shares lost 2%.

item.title: What to know about testosterone replacement therapy as the U.S. military plans to test troops
item.description: While some research has shown benefits for patients who qualify, questions remain about the therapy's long-term risks and use among men without a medical need.

item.title: Iran's Houthi allies declare maritime embargo against Saudi Arabia, escalating threat to oil market
item.description: The militants, in a statement carried by state news, accused the Saudis of laying an "aggressive siege" against them.

item.title: Archer stock rips 20% higher as company unveils military craft with Anduril
item.description: Archer and its eVTOL peers are racing for FAA certification to start flying their air taxis commercially.

item.title: The bar for Tesla earnings is sky-high. Here's why and how options traders can capitalize
item.description: Michael Khouw breaks down how he's trading Tesla into earnings.

item.title: JetBlue wins Spirit slots at LaGuardia Airport, eyes move to Marine Air Terminal as airlines fight for space
item.description: JetBlue Airways won takeoff and landing slots for up to 12 round-trip flights at LaGuardia Airport.

item.title: Here's our advice for investors looking to buy the dip in volatile AI stocks
item.description: When trading in a sector, industry, or stock changes dramatically, as it has with the hyperscalers and AI stocks in recent days, you must change your scales.

item.title: More Americans are taking out personal loans. Here's how to know if you should
item.description: From consolidating debt to covering a major expense, here are three times a personal loan may be worth considering.

item.title: AMD launches Helios, its first rack AI system to rival Nvidia, adding Microsoft as newest buyer
item.description: AMD is taking aim at Nvidia with Helios, its first AI rack system, and has landed Microsoft as a new customer, joining Meta, OpenAI and Oracle.

item.title: Alphabet stock pops on report it's developing a more efficient AI chip
item.description: The new AI chip, called "Frozen v2," would embed parts of Gemini's architecture directly into the silicon, according to the report.

item.title: Voters wary of government ownership in companies as Trump administration takes equity stakes
item.description: About half of voters believe it isn't appropriate for the U.S. government to own stakes in companies, a new CNBC poll found.

item.title: Man with airsoft rifles caused fireworks explosion outside NYC immigration court: MS NOW
item.description: The FBI told CNBC that an individual set off "an incendiary device" outside 26 Federal Plaza, which contains a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement office.

item.title: Coinbase trading results may fall short of expectations, according to prediction markets
item.description: Speculators think in the company's second quarter earnings report, it will show trading volume fell for a third consecutive quarter.

item.title: Bezos backs CuspAI as startup teams up with Nvidia to hunt for chipmaking materials
item.description: CuspAI is part of a new wave of companies looking to use AI to solve challenges in the physical world.

item.title: UK's Andy Burnham becomes PM as Trump slams Britain as 'Poverty Stricken Disaster'
item.description: The prospect of Burnham, considered more left-leaning than Starmer, replacing the incumbent prime minister sent jitters through bond markets earlier this year.

item.title: FBI will no longer investigate ICE confrontations, The New York Times reports
item.description: The Department of Justice and the Department of Homeland Security have both denied that any change in oversight has taken place.
